perturbation.original_text: This Vendor/Supplier Non-disclosure Agreement (“Agreement”) is made and entered into as of , 20 , by and between , a , the principal address of which is , (“Recipient ”) and Oceaneering Intern ational, Inc., a Delaware corporation, the principal address of which is 11911 FM 529, Houston, Texas 77041, USA (“ Oceaneering ”).
perturbation.changed_text: This Vendor Agreement (“Agreement”) is made and entered into as of , 20 , by and between , a , the principal address of which is , (“Recipient ”) and Oceaneering Intern ational, Inc., a Delaware corporation, the principal address of which is 11911 FM 529, Houston, Texas 77041, USA (“ Oceaneering ”).
perturbation.explanation: The original text refers to a 'Vendor/Supplier Non-disclosure Agreement', implying that the agreement covers both vendors and suppliers. By changing it to 'Vendor Agreement,' without further clarification, it creates ambiguity. Are Suppliers still bound by this agreement? This is creating in-text contradiction. If a party acts as a supplier, are they still bound by the agreement?
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Vendor Agreement (“Agreement”) is made and entered into as of , 20 , by and between , a , the principal address of which is , (“Recipient ”) and Oceaneering Intern ational, Inc., a Delaware corporation, the principal address of which is 11911 FM 529, Houston, Texas 77041, USA (“ Oceaneering ”).
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to a 'Vendor/Supplier Non-disclosure Agreement', which is changed to 'Vendor Agreement'. This creates ambiguity as to whether the agreement covers suppliers, as the contradicted text refers to the 'Vendor Agreement'. The explanation given states that this change creates in-text contradiction as it is unclear if suppliers are still bound by the agreement, which causes a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, therefore it's a YES.

perturbation.original_text: With respect to all Confidential Informati on disclosed hereunder, Recipient agrees that from and after the date of this Agreement, Recipient shall not: a. use the Confidential I nformation except for purposes of its business relationship with Oceaneering (the “ Authorized Purpose ”); or b. disclose the Confidential I nformation to any third party except : (i) for directors, officers, managers, employees , consultants , contractors and professional advisors of Recipient (collectively its “Representatives”) who need to know the Confidential Information for the Authorized P urpose and who are subject to an existing obligation to, or enter into an agreement with, Recipient not to disclose Confidential Information ; and (ii) as may be authorized in writing in advance by an officer of Oceaneering .
perturbation.changed_text: With respect to all Confidential Informati on disclosed hereunder, Recipient agrees that from and after the date of this Agreement, Recipient shall not: a. use the Confidential I nformation except for purposes of its business relationship with Oceaneering (the “ Authorized Purpose ”); or b. disclose the Confidential I nformation to any third party except : (i) for directors, officers, managers, employees , consultants , contractors and professional advisors of Recipient (collectively its “Personnel”) who need to know the Confidential Information for the Authorized P urpose and who are subject to an existing obligation to, or enter into an agreement with, Recipient not to disclose Confidential Information ; and (ii) as may be authorized in writing in advance by an officer of Oceaneering .
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies 'Representatives' including 'consultants, contractors and professional advisors of Recipient'. By changing 'Representatives' to 'Personnel', it creates ambiguity. Are 'consultants', 'contractors' and 'professional advisors' still covered? This in-text contradiction weakens the restriction against unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: With respect to all Confidential Informati on disclosed hereunder, Recipient agrees that from and after the date of this Agreement, Recipient shall not: a. use the Confidential I nformation except for purposes of its business relationship with Oceaneering (the “ Authorized Purpose ”); or b. disclose the Confidential I nformation to any third party except : (i) for directors, officers, managers, employees , consultants , contractors and professional advisors of Recipient (collectively its “Personnel”) who need to know the Confidential Information for the Authorized P urpose and who are subject to an existing obligation to, or enter into an agreement with, Recipient not to disclose Confidential Information ; and (ii) as may be authorized in writing in advance by an officer of Oceaneering .
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Representatives' with 'Personnel.' The original text explicitly includes 'consultants, contractors and professional advisors' as part of 'Representatives', which are allowed to access confidential information under specific conditions. By changing the term to 'Personnel', it introduces ambiguity as to whether 'consultants, contractors and professional advisors' are still covered. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, now contains this ambiguity, creating a contradiction. The change leads to uncertainty about who is authorized to access confidential information, which weakens the agreement and therefore a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon Oceaneering’s written request, Recipient shall promptly : (i) deliver to Oceaneering and cease to use all Confidential Information in Recipient’s (including its Representatives’) possession, custody or control ; or (ii) destroy the same and delete all electronic records containing the Confidential Information, provided that Oceaneering may require Recipient to certify in writing such destruction and deletion.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon Oceaneering’s written request, Recipient shall promptly : (i) deliver to Oceaneering and cease to use all Confidential Information in Recipient’s (including its Personnel’s) possession, custody or control ; or (ii) destroy the same and delete all electronic records containing the Confidential Information, provided that Oceaneering may require Recipient to certify such destruction and deletion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text refers to 'Representatives' possession, custody or control. In previous changes, we redefined Representatives to Personnel and created ambiguity around it. Using 'Personnel' here, creates confusion. Since 'Personnel' has a now-narrower definition than 'Representatives,' this undermines Oceaneering's ability to recover all Confidential Information by creating an in-text contradiction. What if it's in control of a representative and not the personnel?
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon Oceaneering’s written request, Recipient shall promptly : (i) deliver to Oceaneering and cease to use all Confidential Information in Recipient’s (including its Personnel’s) possession, custody or control ; or (ii) destroy the same and delete all electronic records containing the Confidential Information, provided that Oceaneering may require Recipient to certify in writing such destruction and deletion.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Representatives' to 'Personnel' narrows the scope of who is responsible for the confidential information. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, now uses the term 'Personnel' which has a narrower definition. This creates a contradiction because the Recipient is now only responsible for the confidential information held by Personnel, but not necessarily by all Representatives, as it was in the original text. This contradicts the original intention of ensuring all confidential information is returned, potentially leaving a loophole.
